name: Bevy Community Events Platform API
what_it_does: Bevy community events management REST API for tech companies to manage distributed developer communities, user groups, and chapter events at scale with volunteer management, global event coordination, and community analytics. Enables AI agents to manage chapter and community creation for global community program automation, handle event creation and RSVP management for chapter event automation, access volunteer and chapter leader management for community program automation, retrieve global community analytics and engagement metrics for community health automation, manage community member directory and networking for community relationship automation, handle sponsorship and budget management for chapter funding automation, access event content and agenda management for programming automation, retrieve community growth and chapter performance reporting for program intelligence automation, manage chapter onboarding and certification for community governance automation, and integrate Bevy with Salesforce, Slack, Zoom, and CRM platforms for end-to-end distributed community event automation.
use_cases: Managing developer user groups from community program agents
Organizing global chapter events from community expansion agents
Tracking community analytics from engagement monitoring agents
Onboarding chapter leaders from community governance agents
not_for: Single-brand consumer communities (use Circle or Mighty Networks for brand communities)
Internal corporate events (use Hopin or Cvent for internal events)
Open public community events without chapter structure (use Meetup or Luma)
best_when: A tech company wanting AI agents to automate global developer community management, chapter event coordination, volunteer leader management, and community program analytics through Bevy's community-as-a-service platform.
avoid_when: SALESFORCE INTEGRATION DEPTH REQUIREMENT: Bevy is backed by Salesforce Ventures and integrates deeply with Salesforce CRM; automated community program management using Bevy-Salesforce integration requires active Salesforce CRM setup; automated community member attribution to sales pipeline requires coordinated Bevy-Salesforce data governance. CHAPTER LEADER PERMISSION GOVERNANCE: Bevy chapter leaders have delegated event management permissions; automated event creation on behalf of chapter leaders must use correct chapter leader credentials; automated central admin event creation assigned to chapters without chapter leader buy-in creates top-down event ownership that undermines community self-governance model. COMMUNITY SCALE PRICING FOR AUTOMATED PROGRAMS: Bevy pricing is based on number of chapters and events; automated community growth programs that rapidly expand chapter count may incur significant additional costs; automated chapter creation must include budget governance to prevent uncontrolled community expansion cost.

auth.notes: Bevy uses API key and OAuth for integrations. REST API with JSON. Palo Alto, California HQ. Founded 2017 by Derek Anderson. Backed by Salesforce Ventures, Sequoia ($40M+ raised). Products: Chapter management, event platform, volunteer management, community analytics, Salesforce integration. GDPR. Serves tech companies with developer communities (Salesforce Ohana, Figma, Atlassian, etc.). Competes with Meetup, Eventbrite, and Hopin for developer community event management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise annual pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Custom enterprise pricing based on number of chapters, events, and members; six-figure annual contracts for large programs
pricing.notes: Palo Alto CA. Salesforce Ventures/Sequoia backed. Enterprise pricing. Annual contract. Chapter-based.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; event and member queries throttled; webhooks for event registration, attendance, and chapter events

known_agent_gotchas: CHAPTER HIERARCHY ORGANIZATION ID CONTEXT: Bevy organizations contain chapters which contain events; automated event management must maintain correct organization ID and chapter ID hierarchy; automated event creation without proper chapter context creates event not visible in chapter's public event calendar

MEMBER vs ATTENDEE vs VOLUNTEER ROLE DISTINCTION: Bevy distinguishes between community members (general program participants), event attendees (RSVP'd for specific event), and volunteers (chapter leaders and helpers); automated community management must use correct endpoint for each role type; automated member provisioning using attendee endpoint creates incomplete community program record

SALESFORCE INTEGRATION REQUIRES CONFIGURED FIELD MAPPING: Bevy-Salesforce integration syncs community data to CRM; automated community-to-sales pipeline attribution requires configured field mapping between Bevy member fields and Salesforce lead/contact fields; automated attribution without configured mapping creates Salesforce records with unmapped community data

EVENT TIMEZONE HANDLING FOR GLOBAL CHAPTERS: Bevy global chapter events span multiple timezones; automated event creation must specify timezone explicitly per event; automated global chapter event creation using UTC without timezone specification creates events displaying in UTC for all chapters regardless of local timezone

COMMUNITY HEALTH METRICS CALCULATION CADENCE: Bevy community analytics (engagement rate, growth rate) are calculated on periodic cadence rather than real-time; automated community health monitoring alerts must account for metric refresh cadence; automated alerts on real-time attendance change may trigger on metric calculation artifact rather than actual community health change
agent_readiness.security_notes: Community events. GDPR, SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US. Community member, event, and chapter data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Community platform. GDPR, SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US. Community event and member data.
